name: Kamo G
tagline: Pretoria-born artist • The Son of Moses
bio:
Born in Pretoria. Raised on the raw edges of Kempton Park. Kamo G has been chasing the sound since he was 9 years old — a kid from the suburbs of Pretoria who found his voice in the space between hip-hop, Afro, and the unfiltered truth of South African life.

At 16, he made the decision: music wasn't just a dream — it was the only path. Growing up in a musical family, the genres blurred together. Gospel. Hip-hop. Afrobeat. Kwaito. It all became part of the DNA that powers every track he makes.

"Son of Moses" isn't just a song title. It's a statement. A declaration of identity. A Pretoria-born artist carrying a story that refuses to be ignored.

From the streets of Tshwane to the stages of Johannesburg, Kamo G is here to make music that speaks to every circle of life — the struggle, the grind, the hope, and the celebration. Every track is a page from his story. The album "Oxygen Tank" is the next chapter.

Kamo G is not here to fit in. He's here to stand out — blending genres, breaking rules, and building something real.

narrative.whySonOfMoses:
The name 'Son of Moses' is rooted in family. It's a tribute to his father, Moses — the man whose name carries the weight of legacy, faith, and identity. Born in Pretoria and raised in Kempton Park, Kamo G grew up in a home where music was everywhere. Gospel. Hip-hop. Afrobeat. Kwaito. It all blended together, and by age 9, he was already chasing the sound.

At 16, he made the choice that would define his life: music wasn't a hobby — it was the path. Every track he creates is a page from his story. 'Son of Moses' is more than a song title. It's a declaration of where he comes from, who raised him, and what he's here to say.

The album 'Oxygen Tank' (released December 2023) is the next chapter — a project that speaks to every circle of life: the struggle, the grind, the hope, and the celebration.
narrative.radioNarrative:
Kamo G (born Kamogelo Nonyane) is a Pretoria-born South African artist raised in Kempton Park. His sound blends hip-hop, Afrobeat, and raw storytelling — shaped by a childhood in a musical family where gospel, hip-hop, and kwaito all played a part.

He discovered music at age 9 and committed to the path at 16. His release 'Son of Moses' — named after his father Moses — is both a personal tribute and an identity statement. The album 'Oxygen Tank' (December 2023) represents the next evolution of his story.

Kamo G's mission is to create music that speaks to all walks of life — rooted in Pretoria, built in Johannesburg, and reaching for the world.

timeline.desc: Kamo G was born in Pretoria, a city that shapes his identity and sound.
Grew up on the raw edges of Kempton Park, where the suburbs met the streets.
Drawn to music from an early age. Growing up in a musical family, the genres blurred together — gospel, hip-hop, Afrobeat, kwaito.
Chose music as a career. Not just a dream — the only path forward.
Started building a catalogue of singles and EPs, blending genres and breaking rules.
A tribute to his father Moses. A declaration of identity. The song that changed everything.
The album released December 2023. A project that speaks to every circle of life — the struggle, the grind, the hope, and the celebration.
The story continues. New music. New stages. Same mission.
